name: microsoft365
supported_scope: Microsoft workplace support issues for Teams, Outlook, OneDrive, Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Windows, and Microsoft account.
description: Default ChatterRax live-demo domain. This pack shows the same JSON-driven structure a replacement domain would use while keeping the built-in Microsoft 365 rules and knowledge active.

client.chat_subtitle: Microsoft workplace support triage and ticket intake.
client.welcome_template: Hi{name_part} I am ChatterRax, a Microsoft 365 bot here to help you out. I can triage Microsoft workplace issues across Teams, Outlook, OneDrive, and more.

teams.label: Teams
teams.follow_up: Tell me whether Teams failed during sign-in, joining a meeting, chat/messages, or audio/video devices.
teams.reply_opener: For Teams, the next step depends on whether this is the meeting, messaging, sign-in, or device path.

outlook.label: Outlook
outlook.follow_up: Tell me whether Outlook failed while sending, receiving, opening mail, searching, using calendar, or signing in.
outlook.reply_opener: For Outlook, the useful split is webmail versus desktop Outlook, then the specific mail action that failed.

onedrive.label: OneDrive
onedrive.follow_up: Tell me whether OneDrive shows a red X, pending sync, missing files, upload failure, or a file that will not open.
onedrive.reply_opener: For OneDrive, start with the sync client state and the exact file status before changing the file.

sharepoint.label: SharePoint
sharepoint.follow_up: Tell me whether SharePoint blocked the site, the file, editing, sync, or the sharing link.
sharepoint.reply_opener: For SharePoint, check the browser view and account permissions before assuming the file itself is damaged.

excel.label: Excel
excel.follow_up: Tell me whether Excel failed while opening, saving, calculating formulas, refreshing data, or formatting.
excel.reply_opener: For Excel, the useful split is whether the issue follows one workbook or every workbook.

word.label: Word
word.follow_up: Tell me whether Word failed while opening, saving, editing, formatting, printing, or signing in.
word.reply_opener: For Word, first check whether the issue follows one document or the Word app itself.

powerpoint.label: PowerPoint
powerpoint.follow_up: Tell me whether PowerPoint failed while opening, presenting, playing media, formatting, or saving.
powerpoint.reply_opener: For PowerPoint, first separate one-deck problems from app-wide problems.

windows.label: Windows
windows.follow_up: Tell me whether Windows is blocking sign-in, display, sound, Bluetooth, printer, update, or another device setting.
windows.reply_opener: For Windows, start with the local setting, device state, or update screen tied to the symptom.

microsoft account.label: Microsoft account
microsoft account.follow_up: Tell me whether the Microsoft account blocker is password, verification code, Authenticator, recovery, or a sign-in loop.
microsoft account.reply_opener: For Microsoft account issues, keep secrets out of chat and test the official sign-in page first.

microsoft 365.label: Microsoft 365
microsoft 365.follow_up: Name the app if you can. If not, tell me what you clicked, what appeared, and any exact code or wording.
microsoft 365.reply_opener: Let us narrow this from the symptom instead of guessing the app.

sign_in.wrap_up: If sign-in still fails, keep the exact wording and do not share passwords or verification codes.

password_reset.wrap_up: If the reset page will not accept your info, keep the exact sign-in message.

sync.wrap_up: If sync still fails, compare the web version with the desktop app before opening a ticket.

email_delivery.wrap_up: If mail still will not send, keep the full bounce-back or send/receive error.

permissions.wrap_up: If access is still blocked, capture the link type and the account shown in the browser.

crash.wrap_up: If it fails again, note whether it happens at launch, one file, or one action.

update.wrap_up: If the same update keeps failing, note the error code and failed update name.

audio.wrap_up: If audio still fails, note the selected input and output devices.

video.wrap_up: If video still fails, test whether the camera works in another app.

printing.wrap_up: If printing still fails, keep the printer name and queue status.

activation.wrap_up: If activation still fails, confirm the license is assigned to the signed-in work account.

status.wrap_up: If status still looks wrong, compare the web app and desktop app.

outage.wrap_up: If many users are affected, include the affected group and when it started.

escalation.wrap_up: If you need hands-on help, include the app, exact symptom, and business impact.

device_setup.wrap_up: If setup still fails, include the device model and exact setup screen.

display.wrap_up: If display still fails, note whether it affects one app or the whole device.

formatting.wrap_up: If formatting still looks wrong, say whether it happens in one file or every file.

notification.wrap_up: If notifications still do not appear, compare app settings and Windows notification settings.

performance.wrap_up: If it is still slow, note whether it affects one app or the whole device.

unknown.wrap_up: If that path does not match what you see, send the exact wording and app screen.

teams|sync: Teams messages getting stuck usually means the desktop app, network, or session cache needs a reset.
Check your internet connection, fully quit Teams, and reopen it.
If messages still will not send, try Teams on the web to see whether the issue is only in the desktop app.
outlook|email_delivery: Outlook send failures usually need the outbox, connection state, and webmail checked first.
Check whether Outlook is in Work Offline mode, then try sending the message from Outlook on the web.
If the message bounces, keep the full bounce-back text because the recipient or domain detail matters.
onedrive|sync: OneDrive being stuck usually means sync needs a clean restart or the account needs to be confirmed.
Confirm you are signed in to the correct work account, then pause and resume sync from the OneDrive cloud icon.
If the file still says pending, reopen OneDrive and check for sync errors.
sharepoint|permissions: SharePoint access errors usually mean the link and the signed-in account do not line up.
Open the file or site link in a browser and confirm you are signed in with the correct work account.
If it still says access denied, ask the file owner to share it again or verify your permissions from SharePoint.
windows|update: Windows Update getting stuck or failing usually means a specific update has an install error or the update service needs a reset.
Go to Settings > Windows Update and click Check for updates, then note the error code shown next to any failed update.
If it is stuck on the same update, run the Windows Update Troubleshooter from Settings > System > Troubleshoot > Other troubleshooters.
microsoft account|sign_in: Microsoft account prompts can loop when the sign-in session or verification method is out of step.
Try signing in at microsoft365.com and confirm whether the verification code or Authenticator prompt appears.
Do not share passwords, verification codes, recovery codes, or Authenticator codes in chat.

knowledge_resources.title: Teams chat messages are not sending
steps: Fully quit Teams and reopen it.
Try sending the same message in Teams on the web.
If web works but desktop fails, clear the Teams app cache or reinstall Teams.

knowledge_resources.title: Outlook email stuck in the outbox
steps: Make sure Outlook is not in Work Offline mode.
Try sending the same message from Outlook on the web.
If the message bounces, keep the full bounce-back text.

knowledge_resources.title: OneDrive sync stuck or pending
steps: Open the OneDrive cloud icon and check the sync activity panel.
Confirm the file opens correctly in OneDrive on the web.

knowledge_resources.title: SharePoint file or site says access denied
steps: Open the link in a private browser window.
Confirm the signed-in account is the expected work account.
Ask the file owner to verify the sharing link and your permission.

knowledge_resources.title: Windows update stuck or showing an error code
steps: Restart once and return to Settings > Windows Update.
Record the failed update name and exact error code.

extra_rules: This domain is Microsoft 365 support. Keep routing inside Teams, Outlook, OneDrive, SharePoint, Excel, Word, PowerPoint, Windows, Microsoft account, or Microsoft 365.
Do not ask users to share passwords, verification codes, recovery codes, or Authenticator codes.
